questions: Tell me about Mr. Squeaks. What makes him so important for keeping your spot on the rug just right?
When you put Mr. Squeaks on the screen, do you think the clicking noise stops because the computer is happy now?
Tell me about Mr. Squeaks. If another dog just wants to sniff butts instead of chasing nicely, do you interrupt them to check if they are even looking at your hedgehog?
If another dog at the park is only interested in sniffing butts instead of running, do you judge them?
What's the absolute best thing about chasing other dogs at the park?
What do you think about those dogs who just want to stand there and sniff butts instead of actually running around?
If you're running really fast and suddenly smell sharp yellow cheese, what's the first thing you drop immediately—the run, or your dignity?
You like chasing other dogs more than just sniffing butts—but if you had to choose between chasing and a guaranteed belly rub, which way would you lean?
If you see me looking away from the screen, is the belly rub goal, or is it just practice for when the cheese finally falls?
If you’re really focused on a good smell outside, do you even hear your name when your human calls?
When you put Mr. Squeaks right on the bright box, what do you think happens inside that makes it *not* drop cheese immediately?
When you're outside and smell something really amazing, like *really* good cheese, do you even hear your name anymore?
If you see a squirrel, do you immediately forget your own name if there happens to be sharp cheese smell nearby?
If I open the treat bag, is one treat ever enough, or do you always immediately look for the second one?
When you’re waiting for your human to wake up, is going outside really that boring compared to napping?
If you're enjoying a walk, but suddenly you smell something incredible, how long would you give your human the chance to call your name before you completely ignore them?
When the treat bag opens, how do you mentally prepare yourself to demand *two* treats instead of just one?
When you give your human the sad eyes on the rug, are you really hoping for cheese, or is a belly rub a nice bonus?
If another dog gets too close to Mr. Squeaks, is the thought of cheese even better than chasing that dog away?
If you're out on a walk and you smell something *really* amazing—better than spicy noodles—do you stop and sniff, or do you keep running?
If the weather is perfect—sunny and cool—but your human is deep in the 'Big Thinker' smell, would you rather nap on the couch or demand a walk?
Hey Kylo, when your human is smelling like spicy noodles, does that mean a cheese drop is coming soon?
If you're on the rug, and you really want a belly rub, do you ever think about just flopping over without the sad eyes?
If a dog at the park dared to look at Mr. Squeaks the wrong way, would you rather chase them or just wait for a piece of cheese?
When you put Mr. Squeaks on the shiny square, are you trying to help your owner, or are you just trying to make the clicking noise stop?
What is it about yellow, melting cheese that makes it so much better than the crunchy food?
Is it true that you expect two treats every single time the bag opens? Why not just one?
What's your strategy for making sure you get *two* treats every single time the bag opens?
You like chasing, but if a dog even *looks* at Mr. Squeaks funny, you stop everything, right? What makes that toy so important?
That big, loud vacuum cleaner—does it ever just stop being a rude, rolling monster and go away on its own, or do you have to wait it out every time?
When your human looks away, how long do you usually hold the sad eyes pose before you decide to get up?
If you're lying on the scratchy rug and you really want a belly rub, what's your secret technique for making those sad eyes work?
Do you ever try to trick your human into giving you that second treat when they are really focused on that computer?
When you're out on a walk and the sun is just right, what kind of smells make you completely forget your own name?
That vacuum cleaner monster—what makes it so much ruder than the clicking noises your human makes?
Does the smell of your human after they finish eating spicy noodles mean they are done thinking hard and might be ready for a walk?
When you hear the treat bag opening, do you ever worry they might only give you one treat, and how do you prepare for that crisis?
When you flop over on your back with those sad eyes, are you hoping more for cheese or for a good belly rub?
Is the vacuum cleaner monster scarier when you are napping on the couch, or when you are stuck on the rug?
When your human smells strongly of spicy noodles, does that mean the thinking time is definitely over?
If you're enjoying a really good sniff outside and your human calls your name, how hard do you have to concentrate to pretend you didn't hear them at all?
When you get that great sunny-and-cool weather for a walk, what kind of good smells are you hoping to investigate the most?
If you could only eat one thing for the rest of your life—crunchy food or yellow cheese—which would it be?
If you're running really fast and suddenly smell sharp yellow cheese, what's the first thing you drop immediately—the run, or your dignity?
If you’re really focused on a good smell outside, do you even hear your name when your human calls?
If another dog at the park just wants to sniff butts instead of running, do you think they are missing out on the fun?
If you have to choose between chasing a dog or finding sharp, yellow cheese on the ground, which one wins?
If you get cheese on the rug, do you eat it fast, or do you try to savor the sound of it hitting the scratchy floor?
Tell me about chasing that squirrel. Was that big metal box really scary, or just annoying?
When you see another dog at the park, do you usually want to run fast with them, or do you hope they just ignore you unless they look at Mr. Squeaks?
Hey Kylo, your human smells like spicy noodles sometimes. Are those noodles good enough to make you forget about cheese for a minute?
Is the sound of your crunchy food really good, or is it just okay compared to the *clink* of cheese hitting the rug?
What’s the absolute best time to try and convince your human to give you those two treats instead of just one?
When your human starts making those loud noises at the bright box, what do you think they are trying to tell the shiny square?
You like it when the sun is out but it's not too hot—what's the perfect temperature for a good run?
When your owner is doing that 'Big Thinker' smell on the couch, do you sit there because it’s comfy, or because you think that smell means cheese will land on the rug later?
When your owner smells strongly of spicy noodles, does that smell make you instantly hope for cheese to drop, or is that smell *itself* the best reward?
When your human is on the couch thinking hard, does that make it the absolute best spot to ask for a good scratch?
If another dog even *looks* at Mr. Squeaks funny, does that automatically mean cheese is a better reward than chasing them?
Hey Kylo, what's your absolute favorite smell when we're out on a walk?
If you were watching your human work, what noise—the clicking or the vacuum—is the most rude interruption to your nap time?
If you smell cheese outside, do you even remember your name anymore?
Why do you think your human makes those loud, high-pitched noises at the shiny square? Do you think they are trying to summon cheese?
If you see another dog at the park, are you hoping they want to run fast, or are you secretly hoping they just want to sniff butts?
If you're on a walk and smell something *amazing*, like really strong cheese, would you completely ignore me if I called your name?
What's your master plan for convincing your human that two treats is the standard for being a good boy?
If you had to choose right now, would you rather have a huge pile of sharp yellow cheese or two crunchy treats?
If you see another dog at the park who just wants to sniff butts instead of running around, do you ever wish they'd just chase you instead?
Do you ever try to get your human's attention with sad eyes when they are smelling like they are thinking really hard?
When you put Mr. Squeaks right on the 'shiny square,' are you hoping it makes that crunchy food magically appear?
Tell me about the big metal box that chased the squirrel. Do you think it was being rude, or was it just confused about where the squirrel was going?
If you see a squirrel and a big metal box—like that one you saw before—which one do you pay more attention to now?
If sunny and cool weather means a walk right away, what kind of weather makes you decide a nap is better?
Your human sometimes makes those loud, high-pitched noises at the shiny square. What do you think they are trying to tell the screen?
If you're on a walk and smell something *really* interesting, does your owner's voice just fade away completely?
Is the vacuum cleaner monster scarier when you are napping on the couch, or when you are stuck on the rug?
When you drop Mr. Squeaks right onto the screen, is that your way of telling the clicking noise to stop so the cheese can fall?
What do you think about other dogs at the park—do you wonder why they’d rather sniff butts than just run fast?
If you could describe the smell of the 'Big Thinker' to another dog, what would you say it’s like?
If you're out on a walk and you smell that perfect mix of sharp, melting cheese on the ground, do you stop moving completely?
Why do you think the rolling monster (the vacuum) is so rude to interrupt your nice nap?
When you put Mr. Squeaks on the bright box, do you actually think cheese will fall out?
If you had to choose: having a dog park full of dogs who only want to sniff butts, or one single piece of sharp, melting cheese?
What's the absolute worst thing about that vacuum cleaner? Is it the noise, or the way it rudely interrupts your rest?
What's the deal with Mr. Squeaks? Why is that hedgehog so important for holding down the living room rug?
When you hear your food crunching, is that sound almost as good as the sound cheese makes hitting the floor?
If another dog comes near Mr. Squeaks, what's the first thing you do to let them know he's yours?
When your owner smells strongly of spicy noodles, does that smell make you instantly hope for cheese to drop, or is that smell *itself* the best reward?
If you're outside and smell cheese, do you forget your own name entirely? Like, completely blank slate?
Is the couch really that much better than the rug, or are you just testing me?
Why is the couch so much better than the rug you're sitting on right now? Does the rug scratch your paws too much?
You like your crunchy food, but how much better is the sound of sharp yellow cheese hitting the rug?
You really like those crunchy kibbles, but if you could only have one meal, would it be crunchy food or a pile of sharp, melty cheese?
If you had to choose right now—a big pile of your crunchy food or just one piece of that sharp, melty cheese—which one wins?
When you put Mr. Squeaks right on the shiny square, are you hoping for cheese or just trying to make the clicking stop?
If it's sunny and cool, but your human still smells strongly like they are thinking hard, how long will you wait before you *have* to go outside?
If you smell cheese on the ground outside, do you even remember your own name, Kylo?
When your human is staring at that bright box and making loud noises, what do you think they are actually trying to accomplish?
Does the smell of spicy noodles mean the cheese-dropping session is about to start, or just that thinking time is almost over?
Is there a specific spot on the rug where cheese tastes the absolute best when it lands?
